name: Auto Math

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## Changes

### PR Review Fixes
- Updated LICENSE year to 2026
- Use `manifest.dir` instead of hardcoded plugin path
- Added comparison with other LaTeX plugins in README
- Added link to wiki documentation

### Code Reorganization
- Reorganized codebase into `src/` directory for better maintainability
- Split main.ts into separate modules: plugin, settings, modals, rules, types, constants, utils
releases.name: Release 0.2.6

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## Main changes
- Improved rules editor with filter, sorting, and unsaved changes indicator.
- Added conflict resolution when multiple rules share the same trigger.
- Updated documentation and version metadata.
releases.name: Release 0.2.5

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
# Changelog

## [0.2.3] - 2025-01-XX

### Changed
- **Breaking:** Changed `\inf` → `\inf_` to prevent conflicts with `\infty`
  - This follows the same pattern as `\lim_` from v0.2.1
  - Allows typing `\infty` without triggering `\inf` expansion
- **Smart Limits now disabled by default** (can be enabled in settings)
- **Smart Limits now applies only to `\int`** (removed `\sum` as it works correctly without explicit `\limits`)

### Technical
- Commented out `\sum` from `SMART_LIMIT_OPERATORS` (kept code for future reference)
- Changed default `smartLimits: false` in settings

### Migration
If you have custom rules using `\inf`, update your `.obsidian/plugins/auto-math/rules.json`:

```json
// Before (v0.2.2):
{ "trigger": "\\inf", "expand": "\\inf_{}" }

// After (v0.2.3):
{ "trigger": "\\inf_", "expand": "\\inf_{}" }
```

## ✅ All Obsidian Review Issues Fixed

Updated `main.ts` with all required fixes:

### Major Changes:
1. ✅ **Vault path:** Now uses `app.vault.configDir` instead of hardcoded `.obsidian`
2. ✅ **Console:** All `console.log` → `console.debug`, default debug OFF
3. ✅ **Commands:** Removed plugin name/ID from commands
4. ✅ **Types:** Replaced all `any` with proper types
5. ✅ **UI:** Using `.setHeading()` for headings
6. ✅ **Promises:** Added `void` operator where needed

Fix all ESLint errors
releases.name: Release 0.2.3

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Fixed
   - Removed misleading ENOENT error on first run when external rules file doesn't exist
   - Changed console.warn to console.log for missing external rules (it's expected behaviour, not a warning)
   - Cleaner console output, especially for new users

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## 🎯 What's New

### Expanded Command Set

Added essential commands for advanced mathematics:

**Fraction Variants:**

- `\dfrac`, `\tfrac`, `\cfrac` — display, text, and continued fractions

**Binomial Coefficients:**

- `\binom`, `\dbinom`, `\tbinom` — binomial coefficient variants

**Limits & Bounds:**

- `\limsup`, `\liminf` — limit superior/inferior
- `\max`, `\min` — maximum/minimum with subscript
- `\inf`, `\sup` — infimum/supremum with subscript

### Breaking Changes

**`\lim` → `\lim_`**

The limit trigger has been changed from `\lim` to `\lim_` to prevent conflicts.

**Before (v0.2.0):**

```
\lim [space] → \lim_{}
```

**Now (v0.2.1):**

```
\lim_ [space] → \lim_{}
```

**Why?** This allows you to type `\lim` directly without triggering expansion when needed.

### Total Commands

- **38 essential commands** now available by default
- Focus on commands that add structure (`{}`, `_{}^{}`, multiline templates)
- Removed simple commands that don't add value (trig functions, etc.)
releases.name: Release 0.2.1

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## v0.2.0 - TypeScript Migration

### 🔧 Technical Changes
- **Migrated to TypeScript** — full type safety for better development experience
- **Build system** — added esbuild for fast compilation
- **Developer setup** — requires Node.js and npm for building

### 📦 For Users
- **No functional changes** — all features work exactly as before
- **Same compatibility** — Obsidian 1.5.0+

### 🛠️ For Developers
To build from source:
```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

### 📥 Installation
Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` from this release and place them in `.obsidian/plugins/auto-math/`
releases.name: Release 0.2.0

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## v0.1.9 - LaTeX Environments

### ✨ New Features
- **LaTeX environment snippets**: Added 9 common environments
- **Multiline templates**: Snippets can now span multiple lines
- Environments: `\align`, `\aligned`, `\gather`, `\cases`, `\array`, `\matrix`, `\pmatrix`, `\bmatrix`, `\split`

### 📝 Example
Type `\align` inside `$$` and it expands to:
```latex
\begin{align}
| ← cursor here
\end{align}
```

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## v0.1.8 - Settings Cleanup

### 🔧 Changes
- Removed "Multiline maths mode" toggle from settings UI
- Multiline mode is now always enabled (no need to toggle)
- Simplified settings interface

### 📝 Technical
- `maxScanLines` remains configurable in `.obsidian/plugins/auto-math/data.json` (default: 50)

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
## v0.1.7 - Multiline Maths Mode

### ✨ New Features
- **Multiline maths mode**: Smart Limits now works inside multiline `$$...$$` blocks

### 🔧 Changes
- Smart Limits now scans neighbouring lines to detect display maths context
- New setting: "Multiline maths mode" (enabled by default)
- Maximum scan depth: 50 lines (configurable)

### 🐛 Fixes
- Fixed Smart Limits not working in multiline display maths blocks

### 📝 Example
```markdown
$$
\int x dx  ← now expands to \int\limits_{}^{}
$$
```
releases.name: Release 0.1.7

releases.author: loglux
releases.description: - **Performance optimisations**: Added early exit checks for faster processing
releases.name: Release 0.1.6

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
Smart Limits update: the former Smart Integrals option is now renamed and expanded.
It now applies to both ∫ and ∑, switching automatically between inline and display forms.
README and settings updated accordingly.
releases.name: Release 0.1.5

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
This update introduces Smart Integrals, a new quality-of-life feature that makes writing mathematical expressions in Obsidian even smoother.

✨ What’s New
🧠 Smart Integrals

Auto Math now automatically chooses the correct integral form when you type \int:

Inside inline math ($…$) → expands to \int_{}^{}

Inside display math ($$…$$) → expands to \int\limits_{}^{}

This behaviour can be toggled in:
Settings → Auto Math → Smart integrals (display vs inline).

various common math operators
improved ordering and sanitation

📝 Documentation updates
README has been updated to reflect the new Smart Integrals feature and revised rule structure.
🔧 Other improvements
Cleaner code structure
More consistent expansion behaviour
Better rule loading from rules.json
Improved developer console logging when debug mode is enabled
releases.name: Release 0.1.4

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
A small but handy update that expands the default rule pack.

🆕 What’s new

Added subscript expansion
New rule:

__  →  _{}


Works as the natural counterpart to ^^ → ^{} for superscripts.
Perfect for quick typing of indices like x__1 → x_{1}.

Improved consistency
Default rule pack now includes both superscript and subscript shortcut
releases.name: Release 0.1.3

releases.author: loglux
releases.description:
This update refines the plugin’s structure and improves rule management.

🆕 What’s new

User rules file relocated
Custom rule definitions are now stored in
.obsidian/plugins/auto-math/auto-math.rules.json
instead of _auto-math.rules.json in the vault root.
(Existing files in the old location will still be used if found.)

Cleaner code & minor fixes

Removed unused parameters from event handlers.

Minor internal refactoring and stability improvements.

💡 Notes

Default (built-in) math pack remains unchanged.
releases.name: Auto Math for Obsidian — v0.1.2

**Updated:**

- Added missing rules to documentation: `\log`, `\ln`, `\sin`, `\cos`, `\tan`, `\cot`, `\sec`, `\csc`, `^^`

**What's included:**
- Default math rule pack (24 LaTeX snippets)
- Custom Rules Editor
- Live rule reload
- Rule file `_auto-math.rules.json`
